rule: Tables can be created manually by defining columns and rows or automatically generated from collections of objects (POCOs).
unit_test: [Fact] public void Table_ShouldSupportManualCreationAndAutomaticPocoGeneration() { var manualTable = new ConsoleTable("Col1", "Col2"); manualTable.AddRow("Val1", "Val2"); Assert.Equal(2, manualTable.Columns.Count); Assert.Single(manualTable.Rows); var pocoList = new List<User> { new User { Name = "John Doe", Age = 30 } }; var autoTable = ConsoleTable.From(pocoList); Assert.Contains("Name", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Contains("Age", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Single(autoTable.Rows); }

rule: Table output and appearance are customizable through predefined visual styles—including Default, Alternative, and Minimal—and configuration settings such as column alignment.
unit_test: [Fact] public void TableStylesAndAlignmentShouldBeCustomizable() { var table = new ConsoleTable("ID", "Value"); table.AddRow(1, "Test"); table.Configure(o => { o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; o.EnableCount = false; }); Assert.Equal(Alignment.Right, table.Options.NumberAlignment); Assert.False(table.Options.EnableCount); var defaultStyle = table.ToString(); var alternativeStyle = table.ToStringAlternative(); var minimalStyle = table.ToMinimalString(); Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(defaultStyle)); Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(alternativeStyle)); Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(minimalStyle)); Assert.NotEqual(defaultStyle, alternativeStyle); Assert.NotEqual(defaultStyle, minimalStyle); }

rule: Table columns must be defined before adding rows, and the number of values in each row must match the number of defined columns.
unit_test: [Fact] public void AddRow_ShouldValidateColumnsAndValueCount() { var table = new ConsoleTable(); var columnException = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("test")); Assert.Equal("Please set the columns first", columnException.Message); table.AddColumn(new[] { "Col1", "Col2" }); var countException = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("only one")); Assert.Contains("does not match the values", countException.Message); }

rule: Cell content exceeding the MaxWidth is automatically wrapped into sub-rows, using WordBreakDelimiters where possible to prevent mid-word splits.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldWrapCellContentAtDelimiterWhenExceedingMaxWidth() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Column1"); table.MaxWidth = 10; table.SetWordBreakDelimiter(' '); table.AddRow("First Second"); var result = table.ToString(); var lines = result.Split(new[] { Environment.NewLine }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries); Assert.Contains("| First    |", result); Assert.Contains("| Second   |", result); Assert.Contains("Count: 2", result); }

rule: Visual width and table alignment calculations must account for Unicode character widths.
Tables can be populated from hierarchical dictionary data structures where outer keys represent row labels.
unit_test: [Fact] public void FromDictionary_ShouldUseOuterKeysAsRowLabelsAndInnerKeysAsColumns() { var data = new Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, object>> { { "RowLabel1", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "ColA", "Data1" }, { "ColB", "Data2" } } }, { "RowLabel2", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "ColA", "Data3" }, { "ColB", "Data4" } } } }; var table = ConsoleTable.FromDictionary(data); Assert.Equal(3, table.Columns.Count); Assert.Equal("", table.Columns[0]); Assert.Equal("ColA", table.Columns[1]); Assert.Equal("ColB", table.Columns[2]); Assert.Equal(2, table.Rows.Count); Assert.Equal("RowLabel1", table.Rows[0][0]); Assert.Equal("Data1", table.Rows[0][1]); Assert.Equal("Data2", table.Rows[0][2]); Assert.Equal("RowLabel2", table.Rows[1][0]); Assert.Equal("Data3", table.Rows[1][1]); Assert.Equal("Data4", table.Rows[1][2]); }

rule: Table output supports mixed data types and correctly handles multi-byte/Unicode character widths.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldSupportMixedDataTypesAndCorrectUnicodeWidths() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Type", "Value").AddRow("Integer", 1).AddRow("Double", 2.5).AddRow("Unicode", "測試 Width").ToString(); Assert.Contains("Integer", table); Assert.Contains("1", table); Assert.Contains("Double", table); Assert.Contains("2.5", table); Assert.Contains("Unicode", table); Assert.Contains("測試 Width", table); var width = ConsoleTable.GetTextWidth("測試 Width"); Assert.Equal(10, width); }

rule: Table display and formatting options, such as numeric column alignment and row count footer visibility, are configurable.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldRespectTableDisplayAndFormattingOptions() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Col1", "Col2"); table.AddRow("Text", 100); table.Configure(options => { options.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; options.EnableCount = false; }); var output = table.ToString(); Assert.DoesNotContain("Count:", output); var lines = output.Split(new[] { '\n', '\r' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries); var dataRow = lines.Last(); Assert.Contains("  100 |", dataRow); }

rule: Table formatting and layout support Unicode character widths, configurable cell alignment, and multiple output styles such as Markdown and standard grids.
unit_test:
[Fact] public void ShouldFormatTableWithUnicodeWidthsAndMultipleStyles() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Character", "Value"); table.AddRow("哈哈", 1).AddRow("WideChars", 200); table.Configure(o => { o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; o.EnableCount = false; }); var gridOutput = table.ToString(); var markdownOutput = table.ToMarkDownString(); Assert.Contains("哈哈", gridOutput); Assert.Contains(" 1 ", gridOutput); Assert.Contains("| 哈哈      |     1 |", markdownOutput.Replace("
", "
")); Assert.Contains("| WideChars |   200 |", markdownOutput.Replace("
", "
")); }

rule: Table output is directed to the standard system console by default.
unit_test: [Fact] public void OutputShouldDefaultToConsoleOut() { var users = new List<User> { new() { Name = "Alexandre", Age = 36 } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(users); Assert.Equal(table.Options.OutputTo, Console.Out); }

rule: Tables can be populated from various data sources including Lists, Dictionaries, DataTables, and Object Arrays.
unit_test: [Fact] public void PopulateFromVariousDataSourcesShouldWork() { var users = new List<User> { new User { Name = "Alex", Age = 30 } }; var tableFromList = ConsoleTable.From(users); Assert.NotNull(tableFromList); Assert.Contains("Name", tableFromList.Columns); var dict = new Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, object>> { { "Row1", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Col1", true } } } }; var tableFromDict = ConsoleTable.FromDictionary(dict); Assert.NotNull(tableFromDict); Assert.Contains("Col1", tableFromDict.Columns); var dt = new System.Data.DataTable(); dt.Columns.Add("Id", typeof(int)); dt.Rows.Add(1); var tableFromDt = ConsoleTable.From(dt); Assert.NotNull(tableFromDt); Assert.Equal("Id", tableFromDt.Columns[0]); var array = new object[][] { new object[] { "Header1", "Header2" }, new object[] { "Val1", "Val2" } }; var tableFromArray = ConsoleTable.From(array); Assert.NotNull(tableFromArray); Assert.Equal(2, tableFromArray.Columns.Count); }

rule: Table structure requires columns to be defined before rows are added, and row data must match the established column count.
unit_test: [Fact] public void AddRow_ShouldEnforceColumnDefinitionAndCount() { var table = new ConsoleTable(); var exception1 = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("data")); Assert.Equal("Please set the columns first", exception1.Message); table.AddColumn(new[] { "Col1", "Col2" }); var exception2 = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("only one value")); Assert.Contains("does not match the values", exception2.Message); table.AddRow("Value 1", "Value 2"); Assert.Single(table.Rows); }
